list.title: RepSelect: Robust LLM Unlearning via Representation Selectivity

extra.desc: RepSelect isolates forget-set-specific representations in LLMs by collapsing top principal components of weight gradients, achieving deeper and more robust unlearning compared to existing methods.

list.title: RefGC-SR^2: Reference-guided Generated Content Super-Resolution and Refinement

extra.desc: A new reference-guided generated content super-resolution-refinement task is introduced that simultaneously recovers high-resolution details and refines generative artifacts using a frequency-aware diffusion transformer model.

list.title: Text-Vision Co-Instructed Image Editing

extra.desc: A unified text-visual image editing framework is presented that combines semantic intent from textual instructions with spatial guidance from visual prompts to achieve more precise and faithful image manipulation.

list.title: Learning from the Self-future: On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs

extra.desc: d-OPSD introduces a novel on-policy self-distillation framework for diffusion language models by adapting self-teacher construction and supervision mechanisms to match the non-autoregressive nature of diffusion models.

list.title: Rethinking the Role of Efficient Attention in Hybrid Architectures

extra.desc: Hybrid architectures combining full attention with efficient attention modules like sliding-window attention exhibit distinct scaling behaviors and optimization trajectories, with efficient attention primarily affecting the emergence speed of long-context capabilities rather than final performance.

list.title: ChLogic: Evaluating Robustness of Logical Reasoning in Chinese Expressions

extra.desc: ChLogic benchmark reveals persistent performance gaps between English and Chinese logical reasoning in large language models, influenced by surface realization differences and translation artifacts.

list.title: MotionVLA: Vision-Language-Action Model for Humanoid Motion

extra.desc: A dual-stream frequency tokenizer and autoregressive model are proposed to improve humanoid motion generation by separately encoding pose and physical dynamics, achieving better diversity and consistency compared to single-codebook approaches.

list.title: Show the Signal, Hide the Noise: Spectral Forcing for Pixel-Space Diffusion

extra.desc: Spectral Forcing, a time-conditional 2D-DCT low-pass operator, improves diffusion model efficiency by explicitly separating signal from noise in pixel-space models.

list.title: ACE-Ego-0: Unifying Egocentric Human and Robotic Data for VLA Pretraining

extra.desc: A unified Vision-Language-Action pretraining framework leverages heterogeneous data sources including human egocentric videos and robot trajectories through a reliability-aware training approach that improves performance on embodied AI tasks.

list.title: Variable-Width Transformers

extra.desc: A novel transformer architecture with nonuniform width allocation across layers achieves better performance and efficiency compared to uniform designs by utilizing a parameter-free residual resizing mechanism.

list.title: ProCUA-SFT Technical Report

extra.desc: Training computer-use agents using a large-scale synthetic dataset with automated task generation and verification achieves significantly improved performance on desktop interaction benchmarks.

list.title: LoopCoder-v2: Only Loop Once for Efficient Test-Time Computation Scaling

extra.desc: Parallel loop Transformers achieve better code generation performance with two loops due to refined representations, while additional loops cause diminishing returns and increased positional mismatch costs.

list.title: Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization: Teacher in Prompts, Not Gradients

extra.desc: Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization (ZPPO) improves knowledge distillation by using reformulated prompts that help students learn from both correct and incorrect responses, enhancing performance especially at smaller model sizes.

list.title: OPD-Evolver: Cultivating Holistic Agent Evolver via On-Policy Distillation

extra.desc: OPD-Evolver is a self-evolving agent framework that combines slow-fast co-evolution with on-policy self-distillation to enhance memory management and policy learning across multiple domains.

list.title: Beyond Monolingual Deep Research: Evaluating Agents and Retrievers with Cross-Lingual BrowseComp-Plus

extra.desc: Research agents face significant challenges when evidence is in a different language than the query, with performance degrading even when gold evidence is provided directly.

list.title: A Gradient Perspective on RLVR Stability and Winner Advantage Policy Optimization

extra.desc: Training instability in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards is analyzed through token-level gradient dynamics, leading to a stable policy optimization method that updates only on positive-advantage completions.

list.title: GameCraft-Bench: Can Agents Build Playable Games End-to-End in a Real Game Engine?

extra.desc: End-to-end game generation presents significant challenges for coding agents, requiring them to create complete playable games from natural language descriptions while meeting specific evaluation criteria for engine grounding, artifact completeness, and interactive verification.

list.title: ActWorld: From Explorable to Interactive World Model via Action-Aware Memory

extra.desc: ActWorld extends navigation-centric interactive world models to support object interaction through a chunk-autoregressive framework with hierarchical action-aware memory and persistent memory banks.

list.title: Looped World Models

extra.desc: Looped World Models introduce iterative latent state refinement through shared transformer blocks, achieving 100x parameter efficiency while adapting computational depth to prediction complexity.

list.title: TRIAGE: Dialectical Reasoning for Explainable Risk Prediction on Irregularly Sampled Medical Time Series with LLMs

extra.desc: A framework called TRIAGE is proposed to improve clinical early warning systems by training large language models to generate dialectical reasoning for continuous risk scoring with better calibration and interpretability.

list.title: LectūraAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Personalized AI-Assisted Learning and Embodied Teaching

extra.desc: LectūraAgents is a multi-agent framework that enables personalized learning through adaptive embodied teaching by mimicking professor-student interactions and generating coordinated teaching actions aligned with learner profiles.

list.title: Unified Multimodal Autoregressive Modeling with Shared Context-Visual Tokenizer is Key to Unification

extra.desc: UniAR presents a unified autoregressive framework that uses a single discrete visual tokenizer to bridge visual understanding and generation, achieving state-of-the-art results in image generation and editing through multi-level feature fusion, bitwise quantization, and parallel prediction.

list.title: Aligning Quantum Operators with Large Language Models

extra.desc: Large language models can be adapted to understand quantum operators by mapping unitary matrices into their latent space, enabling quantum circuit synthesis and language-conditioned gate constraint specification.

list.title: Attacks on Machine-Text Detectors Retain Stylistic Fingerprints

extra.desc: Machine-text detection remains challenging despite evasion techniques, but stylistic features can provide robust defense when analyzed across multiple documents rather than individual instances.

list.title: You Don't Need Strong Assumptions: Visual Representation Learning via Temporal Differences

list.title: Track2View: 4D-Consistent Camera-Controlled Video Generation via Paired 3D Point Tracks

extra.desc: Track2View generates novel camera viewpoints from videos by using 3D point tracks to establish explicit spatiotemporal correspondences, achieving superior visual quality and camera accuracy compared to existing methods.

list.title: ExpRL: Exploratory RL for LLM Mid-Training

list.title: Human Universal Grasping

list.title: Prompt-Level Distillation: A Non-Parametric Alternative to Model Fine-Tuning for Efficient Reasoning
